examples.title: Stop-and-Wait Sender Algorithm
examples.description: Implements sender side of Stop-and-Wait ARQ flow control
examples.code:
PROCEDURE StopAndWaitSender(frameNumber)
 WHILE moreFramesToSend DO
 SEND frame with sequenceNumber
 START timer
 WHILE NOT timeout AND NOT ACKReceived DO
 WAIT for acknowledgment
 END WHILE
 IF ACKReceived THEN
 sequenceNumber ← 1 - sequenceNumber 
 // Toggle between 0 and 1
 STOP timer
 ELSE 
 // Timeout - retransmit
 RETRANSMIT frame with sequenceNumber
 RESET and START timer
 END IF
 END WHILE
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: The sender transmits one frame, starts a timer, and waits for ACK. If ACK arrives, it toggles the sequence number and sends the next frame. If timeout occurs, the same frame is retransmitted.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for n frames - each frame sent once or retransmitted
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) - only stores current frame and sequence number

examples.title: Go-Back-N Sender Algorithm
examples.description: Sliding window sender with cumulative ACKs
examples.code:
CONSTANT N 
// Window size
CONSTANT MAXSEQ 
// Maximum sequence number
VARIABLE sendBase, nextSeqNum

PROCEDURE SendFrame(frame)
 IF nextSeqNum < sendBase + N THEN
 SEND frame[nextSeqNum]
 IF sendBase == nextSeqNum THEN
 START timer for nextSeqNum
 END IF
 nextSeqNum ← nextSeqNum + 1 MOD (MAXSEQ + 1)
 ELSE
 WAIT 
// Window full - must wait
 END IF
END PROCEDURE

PROCEDURE HandleACK(ackNumber)
 IF ackNumber >= sendBase THEN
 sendBase ← ackNumber + 1 MOD (MAXSEQ + 1)
 IF sendBase == nextSeqNum THEN
 STOP timer
 ELSE
 RESTART timer for sendBase
 END IF
 END IF
END PROCEDURE

PROCEDURE Timeout()
 // Retransmit all unacknowledged frames
 FOR i FROM sendBase TO nextSeqNum - 1 DO
 RETRANSMIT frame[i] MOD (MAXSEQ + 1)
 END FOR
 RESTART timer
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: The sender maintains sendBase (left edge of window) and nextSeqNum (next frame to send). It can send up to N frames before waiting. On cumulative ACK, window slides forward. On timeout, all unacknowledged frames are retransmitted.
examples.timeComplexity: O(W) where W is window size for each transmission round
examples.spaceComplexity: O(N) - buffer for up to N frames in window

examples.title: Selective Repeat Receiver Algorithm
examples.description: Receiver that accepts out-of-order frames and individually acknowledges them
examples.code:
CONSTANT N 
// Window size
VARIABLE receiveBase
ARRAY received[N] 
// Buffer for received frames

PROCEDURE ReceiveFrame(frame)
 seqNum ← frame.sequenceNumber
 
 // Check if frame is within receive window
 IF seqNum >= receiveBase AND seqNum < receiveBase + N THEN
 received[seqNum MOD N] ← frame
 SEND ACK for seqNum
 
 // Deliver consecutive frames to network layer
 WHILE received[receiveBase MOD N] IS NOT EMPTY DO
 DELIVER received[receiveBase MOD N] TO networkLayer
 received[receiveBase MOD N] ← EMPTY
 receiveBase ← receiveBase + 1 MOD (MAXSEQ + 1)
 END WHILE
 
 ELSE IF seqNum < receiveBase THEN
 // Duplicate or old frame - send ACK anyway
 SEND ACK for seqNum
 ELSE
 // Frame outside window - discard silently
 END IF
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: The receiver maintains a buffer for frames within its window. When an in-order frame arrives at the window base, it delivers all consecutive buffered frames to the network layer and advances the window. Duplicate frames outside the window are acknowledged but discarded.
examples.timeComplexity: O(N) for window management and delivery
examples.spaceComplexity: O(N) - buffer for entire receive window
